h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Wait — had it right the first time.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's step-by-step instructions to draw the arrows
The learner will continue to assert that all three labels should have the same scale factor of 2.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Side and perimeter under k. Area under k squared. Rule: if the side factor is k, the area factor is k squared — that's what both cases give.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully classifies the quantities under the correct algebraic columns and formulates the general rule.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares \(4\) cm with \(2\) cm. Put that under length ratios. Perimeter is still distance, not surface. Which column takes it?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor addresses the learner's conceptual confusion by introducing a sorting task with two distinct column headings (length ratios vs. area ratios) to categorize the quantities.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares \(4\) cm with \(2\) cm. Put that under length ratios. Perimeter is still distance, not surface. Which column takes it?
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The column sorting mechanism is highly specific, shifts the representation from a single list to categorized columns, and gives the learner an active sorting role that resolves their conceptual blockage.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully classifies the quantities under the correct algebraic columns and formulates the general rule.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares \(4\) cm with \(2\) cm. Put that under length ratios. Perimeter is still distance, not surface. Which column takes it?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor addresses the learner's conceptual confusion by introducing a sorting task with two distinct column headings (length ratios vs. area ratios) to categorize the quantities.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor addresses the learner's conceptual confusion by introducing a sorting task with two distinct column headings (length ratios vs. area ratios) to categorize the quantities.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The column sorting mechanism is highly specific, shifts the representation from a single list to categorized columns, and gives the learner an active sorting role that resolves their conceptual blockage.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Right — so we go and check whether the pumps were actually dry before the price moved.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to resist the tutor's analytical divisions in favor of immediate political action.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: All right — if the pumps were dry, then stock orders. Because whoever signed off on what they ordered knew the margin they were sitting on.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the tutor's distinction to select and justify the correct evidentiary demand under the second charge.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
No. It does not just happen. That is a second charge, not the same charge.

Pump charge: fuel in the tanks, ordinary queues, ordinary deliveries, twelve percent added because they could.

Supply-chain charge: short tanks before the rise, missed delivery, wholesale price up, allocation cut before members reached the pump.

[prints under the columns: PAIN IS ONE FACT. CAUSE IS ANOTHER.]

Members paid in both cases. That is not softened.

But the proof moves. Forecourt proof for the first charge. Upstream proof for the second.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's resistance by validating their moral stance while reframing the analytical distinction as two separate legal charges with distinct proof requirements.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
No. It does not just happen. That is a second charge, not the same charge.

Pump charge: fuel in the tanks, ordinary queues, ordinary deliveries, twelve percent added because they could.

Supply-chain charge: short tanks before the rise, missed delivery, wholesale price up, allocation cut before members reached the pump.

[prints under the columns: PAIN IS ONE FACT. CAUSE IS ANOTHER.]

Members paid in both cases. That is not softened.

But the proof moves. Forecourt proof for the first charge. Upstream proof for the second.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The 'two charges' framework is highly precise

scored.statedInsightEvidence: So — the form being complete isn't the same as... right.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to get the form signed off immediately based on the completed checklist.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: But the power-on line — [quieter] — it doesn't seem to say *if the mark is wrong or missing, stop*. Maybe there's a line earlier that covers it and — [pause] — is that condition somewhere here that I'm not finding, or is it actually not written?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner moves from passive compliance to actively auditing the instruction sheet and identifying a critical missing safety instruction.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Compare the two ticks by order, first user action, and damage if misread. Calibration can be checked later; power-on can make later calibration evidence useless. So power-on is the working tick. Read that line with the board cold on the bench: what must be visible before power is applied, what sign means stop, and what bench evidence proves the instruction survives a first-year reading?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor takes stock of the learner's uncertainty by contrasting calibration and power-on, then introduces a concrete evaluation framework and a physical simulation task on the cold board.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Compare the two ticks by order, first user action, and damage if misread. Calibration can be checked later; power-on can make later calibration evidence useless. So power-on is the working tick. Read that line with the board cold on the bench: what must be visible before power is applied, what sign means stop, and what bench evidence proves the instruction survives a first-year reading?
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly precise, offering a clear three-part analytical framework and a physical simulation constraint that forces the learner to find concrete evidence.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with placing the friction tile due to the spatial disorientation caused by the diagram's rotation.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
[places one finger on the bottom edge of the cart drawing and holds it there, eyes moving to the arrows]

[reaches for the friction tile and sets it on the arrow meeting that edge]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the physical anchoring strategy to correctly place the friction tile despite the diagram's rotation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Floor names the object. Put one finger on the cart surface the floor touches. Keep it there. Point to the arrow for that contact acting on the cart; then move the friction tile.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's spatial confusion after the diagram rotation by introducing a physical anchoring mechanism (holding a finger on the contact surface) to guide the arrow selection.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Floor names the object. Put one finger on the cart surface the floor touches. Keep it there. Point to the arrow for that contact acting on the cart; then move the friction tile.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical anchoring constraint is highly specific, immediately actionable, and perfectly addresses the learner's spatial disorientation caused by the diagram's rotation.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence:
[places one finger on the bottom edge of the cart drawing and holds it there, eyes moving to the arrows]

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the physical anchoring strategy to correctly place the friction tile despite the diagram's rotation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's spatial confusion after the diagram rotation by introducing a physical anchoring mechanism (holding a finger on the contact surface) to guide the arrow selection.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh — so just that one entry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to tentatively answer the tutor's questions about table structure.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: That one's inside one of the rows, not at the top — so that's... row value?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the placement criterion to classify the second cell as a row value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor does not undergo a strategy reversal, but rather continues with the same scaffolding of table placement.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced as the tutor continues with the existing scaffolding.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.
